chain.thought: Explanation: By comparing the "age at start date" values, the youngest was Theodore Roosevelt at 49 years, 241 days when he became the oldest living president.

judge_thought: Explanation: The table lists ages at the start date for each person when they became the oldest living president. The youngest age shown is Theodore Roosevelt at 49 years, 241 days (June 24, 1908). All other ages at start date are higher (e.g., Grant 53, Taft 51, Washington 57, etc.). Therefore, the youngest living president (in the context of this table) is Theodore Roosevelt.
